title: Apple Silicon MacBook Master Diagnostic Triage Flow
source: Cross-referenced from 17 board schematics + Eason (iphone_cpu_fix) M1 reference + SAF diagnostic data

does_NOT_work_on: A2179 (MacBook Air 13" 2020 Intel) — IMVP9 architecture, no SVR AON
applicability.reason_intel_excluded: Intel boards use IMVP9 VR + discrete PMIC, no PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN rail, no comparator gating circuit, no SVR AON. Completely different power architecture requiring different diagnostic methodology.

recommended: Soldering iron with fine tip (for comparator jumper)

comparator_lookup.description: The comparator IC that gates PMU_VDD_HI varies by generation. This is the IC you need to jumper for the forced start bypass.

forced_start_bypass.source: Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix — 'How to bypass protective isolation and forced start of 20V boost circuit'

universal_procedure.step_1: Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (use 4.3V if display is connected — display draws enough to sag 3.8V)
universal_procedure.step_2: Force PMU_VDD_HI high — method varies by board (see comparator_lookup above)
universal_procedure.step_3: Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds
universal_procedure.safety_check: Before injecting 3.8V, measure PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN resistance to ground. If near-zero, there is a dead short — find and fix it first before injecting power.

board_boots.fault_range: CD3217 port controllers, charger IC (U5200), fuses, SPI ROM, 20V boost circuit, MagSafe path
board_boots.next_step: Check Steps 1-2 of board-specific diagnostic tree

board_does_not_boot.next_step: Check Steps 3+ of board-specific diagnostic tree

diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Active-low fault flag from SVR AON controller (U5700). LOW = SVR has fault (overcurrent/overvoltage/thermal). HIGH = SVR healthy.
Goes HIGH only after MPMU completes full startup sequence. If PP3V8_AON is present but this stays low, fault is inside PMU, clocks, or SoC handshake.
diagnostic_shortcuts.eliminates: Jumps past power section to compute section

diagnostic_shortcuts.description: PMU_REFCLK32K_OUT — MPMU cannot start without this clock. No oscillation = dead crystal = board won't start.
Forces DFU mode bypassing macOS boot entirely. If DFU works but won't boot normally, power delivery and SoC hardware are fine — problem is NAND/firmware.
diagnostic_shortcuts.eliminates: All hardware faults if DFU succeeds

diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Measure voltage drop across RS710/RS711/RS712 sense resistors while SVR runs. Zero on one while others read equal = dead phase.
diagnostic_shortcuts.eliminates: Identifies exact failed phase without IC removal
diagnostic_shortcuts.present_on: All Apple Silicon boards (3-phase SVR AON)

diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Goes high when charger IC recognizes valid power source. 5V at USB-C but SYS_DETECT low = CD3217 fault. No 5V = cable/connector/port controller.
diagnostic_shortcuts.eliminates: Splits entire charger input path in one probe

diagnostic_shortcuts.description: PP5V_S2 feeds back to PP3V8_AON VDRV after hard start. If board starts then dies after ~1 second, bootstrap path is broken. Check R5900 continuity.
PBUS E-Fuse is a one-time overcurrent protection device. If blown, no power gets through regardless of charger health. Continuity check across 2 pads.
Pro/Max boards have BOM-selectable SVR AON current limits (e.g., 30A vs 42A). Wrong BOM resistors = premature OCP under load. Check BOM resistor values against board variant.
diagnostic_shortcuts.eliminates: Explains intermittent crashes under heavy GPU/CPU load
diagnostic_shortcuts.present_on: Pro/Max boards only (A2442, A2485, A2779, A2780)

diagnostic_shortcuts.description: M3 added Q5960 MOSFET for active throttling (M1/M2 don't have this). If board throttles severely but doesn't crash, Q5960 may be partially failed or mis-biased.

master_triage_flow.description: Follow this flow for ANY no-power/no-boot Apple Silicon MacBook. Covers ~90% of fault scenarios in under 5 minutes.

flow.action: Board on DCPS at 20V — note ammeter reading

branches.normal_boot: Board works — test peripherals (Step 8 of board tree)

branches.sys_detect_low: Port/cable/CD3217 fault — check PPVBUS_USBC, USB-C connectors, SPI ROM

branches.fault_l_low: SVR AON fault — check phase balance (RS710/711/712), thermal cam on U5700 area
branches.fault_l_high: Do FORCED START BYPASS — see comparator_lookup for your board
branches.bypass_boots: FRONT END fault — CD3217, charger IC, fuses, boost circuit
branches.bypass_no_boot: Check 32.768kHz crystal, then PMU_ACTIVE_READY, then SoC/ROM

branches.pp3v8_missing: SVR AON fault — same as C/FAULT_L check
branches.pmu_ready_low: PMU not starting — check 32.768kHz clock, PMU_VDD_HI (comparator), PMU_ONOFF_L
branches.pmu_ready_high: PMU alive — check downstream: PP5V_S2, PP3V3_S2, NAND power

branches.pp5v_s2_missing: PMU alive but PP5V_S2 regulator fault — check P5VS2_PWR_EN, UC300/equivalent
branches.pp5v_s2_present: Board reaching S2 state — check SoC power domains, SPI NOR flash, try DFU mode

branches.dfu_works: Hardware is fine — NAND/firmware issue. Restore or replace NAND.
branches.dfu_fails: SoC or compute path fault — check SoC power domains, ground connections, secure element

flow.action: Board is booting but display path failed
check: Connect external display via HDMI/USB-C to confirm macOS is running
Check backlight driver (BEN/DP600/Luxe depending on model)
Check eDP connector and display power sequencer
Check display flex cable for damage
If Pro/Max: check HDMI output via Madea/Cobra controller

power_on_sequence_universal.source: Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix, verified against all Apple Silicon schematics
power_on_sequence_universal.note: This sequence is the SAME for all Apple Silicon MacBooks. Only component designators and current ratings change between models.
sequence: 1. USB-C/MagSafe provides 5V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPHV_INT → PPDCIN_AONSW
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → charger IC (U5200) with SYS_DETECT from battery connector
3. Charger IC → PPBUS_AON (~12.6V temporary from battery, or direct from adapter)
4. PPBUS_AON → U5700 SVR AON controller → 3-phase buck (Q5800/Q5820/Q5840 via L5800/L5820/L5840) → PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN
5. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → MPMU + comparator circuit. Comparator checks PPBUS_AON via voltage divider. When threshold met → PMU_VDD_HI → MPMU starts
6. MPMU → all PMU rails (buck converters + LDOs). SPMU → additional rails. PP3V3_S2_UPC/PP1V8_S2 to CD3217.
7. SoC hard start — needs 24MHz crystal + 32.768kHz crystal + PMU_RESET_L release. SoC reads ROM, initializes I2C to CD3217.
8. SoC → PMU → P5VS2_PWR_EN → PP5V_S2 regulator starts (AFTER hard start, not during).
9. CD3217 reads shared SPI ROM, sends PD negotiation via CC1/CC2 → charger boosts to 20V.
10. 20V → CD3217 MOSFET → charger IC → real PPBUS_AON (12.6V regulated). System now running at full power.

notes: No HDMI, no SD card slot (except A2918)
SVR AON is 42A+ with BOM-selectable thresholds
Additional VRs for CPU/GPU clusters: Viper+Monaco (M1 Pro/Max) or Austringer+CLVR (M2 Pro/Max)
Enhanced comparator: U5950 + Q5960 active throttle MOSFET
Active throttling instead of hard shutdown on overcurrent
PMU AMR Test circuit with R8020
